questions.question:
A discrete random variable X has the following probability distribution:
P(X = 1) = 0.2, P(X = 2) = 0.3, P(X = 3) = 0.5
Calculate E(X), the expected value of X.
questions.hint: Multiply each outcome by its probability and sum the results.

questions.question: A computer program's execution time, T (in milliseconds), follows a uniform distribution between 10 ms and 50 ms. Calculate the expected execution time E(T).
questions.hint: For a continuous uniform distribution U(a, b), the expected value is (a + b)/2.

questions.question:
A game developer creates a loot box system where a player can win one of three items:
- Common item (value: 10 coins) with probability 0.7
- Rare item (value: 50 coins) with probability 0.2
- Legendary item (value: 200 coins) with probability 0.1
Calculate the expected value (in coins) a player receives from opening one loot box. If the loot box costs 25 coins to open, should a player expect to gain or lose coins in the long run?
questions.hint: First calculate E[Value]. Then compare this to the cost to determine if it's a net gain or loss.
